levels.body: Polony is a large dried sausage made originally of a mixture of pork and other meats, partly-cooked. It probably is a variant or alteration of another lexical item: Bologna sausage ("Polony, *n*.1").
worksCited: Dickens, Charles. "The Chimes." *A Christmas Carol and Other Christmas Books*, edited by Robert Douglas-Fairhurst, OUP, 2008, pp. 85-162.
"Polony, *n*.1" *Oxford English Dictionary*, Oxford UP, https://doi.org/10.1093/OED/6923895554. Accessed 6 January 2024.

levels.body: Polonies, as opposed to trotters, pettitoes and tripe, which are later mentioned by Toby, are based on meat instead of offal.
worksCited: Dickens, Charles. "The Chimes." *A Christmas Carol and Other Christmas Books*, edited by Robert Douglas-Fairhurst, OUP, 2008, pp. 85-162.

levels.body: Toby's succession of guesses starting from a meat-based food, polonies, and then proceeding with offal-based foods, mirrors the typical food of the Victorian poor (cf. Mejer 174).
worksCited: Dickens, Charles. "The Chimes." *A Christmas Carol and Other Christmas Books*, edited by Robert Douglas-Fairhurst, OUP, 2008, pp. 85-162.

Mejer, Anna. The Uses of Food in Charles Dickens' *Novels as a Commentary on Victorian Society and Manners*. 2018. University of Warsaw, PhD dissertation. University of Warsaw Repository, https://depotuw.ceon.pl/handle/item/4228.
